questions.question: What is the primary purpose of chain codes in boundary preprocessing?
options: To identify convex regions
To follow the boundary of a shape
To detect holes
To calculate the area of a shape
questions.explanation: Chain codes are used to follow the boundary of a shape and represent the direction of each pixel.

questions.question: Which of the following is a result of applying boundary following to a shape?
options: The shape becomes more complex
The shape becomes simpler
The shape becomes thinner
The shape becomes thicker
questions.explanation: Boundary following results in a thinner shape as it removes the boundary pixels.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a chain code and a binary image?
options: Chain code is a binary image
Chain code is a grayscale image
Chain code is a vector image
Chain code represents the direction of pixels
questions.explanation: Chain codes are a way to represent the direction of pixels in a shape, whereas binary images represent pixel values as either 0 or 255.

questions.question: Which of the following is a limitation of chain codes?
options: They can be used to detect holes
They can be used to calculate the area of a shape
They can be used to identify convex regions
They do not provide information about pixel values
questions.explanation: Chain codes do not provide information about pixel values, making them limited in terms of analysis and processing.

questions.question: What is the effect of applying boundary following to a shape with a single pixel?
options: The shape becomes thicker
The shape becomes thinner
The shape remains the same
The shape becomes more complex
questions.explanation: Applying boundary following to a shape with a single pixel results in a thinner shape.
